quizSrc.term: What kind of lens do you suppose was used in this photo? [img:PoFPxmukEeeO-Q4_wz24ug_a84c2aa12478443ca6800b9306b5ae56_pexels-photo-87293.jpeg] A. wide angle B. ¨normal¨ or 50mm lens C. long or telefoto lens
quizSrc.definition: C. long or telefoto lens

quizSrc.term: Identify the stylistic element of photography that is dominant in this photo: [img:uxOSGWukEee_LBKKxRRLTg_d78a8cdd4a9b25e89a1520df688d60a2_forest-trees-path-pipe.jpg] A. patterns B. lines C. shapes D. texture
quizSrc.definition: B. lines

quizSrc.term: Identify the stylistic element of photography that is dominant in this photo: [img:GJPhX2ulEeeO-Q4_wz24ug_c6b4d074b93b76017f04690c4fbc83ba_pexels-photo-268883.jpeg] A. patterns B. shapes C. lines D. texture
quizSrc.definition: D. texture

quizSrc.term: Identify the stylistic element of photography that is dominant in this photo: [img:Z55og2ulEee_LBKKxRRLTg_7cd3622fe0cec6f2a8a55683c8d442b8_photo-1442406964439-e46ab8eff7c4.jpg] A. patterns B. lines C. shapes D. texture
quizSrc.definition: A. patterns

quizSrc.term: Identify the stylistic element of photography that is dominant in this photo: [img:l92jZ2ulEeedjgoGzm8emA_22a218b6d722cc6c9c52f11056b0904c_pexels-photo.jpeg] A. rule of thirds B. symmetry C. color D. cold light
quizSrc.definition: C. color

quizSrc.term: Identify the stylistic element of photography that is dominant in this photo: [img:2PMqFGulEeeO-Q4_wz24ug_a9870321dc2f3ea13177e2f2aa3ae39d_pexels-photo-90333.jpeg] A. rule of thirds B. soft light C. symmetry D. hard light
quizSrc.definition: C. symmetry

quizSrc.term: Identify the stylistic element of photography that is dominant in this photo: [img:GHPhs2umEee0ohLeDr_NVg_e20a362c817179dd0ed168ae56160c9f_pexels-photo-241520.jpeg] A. hard light B. soft light C. cool light D. warm light
quizSrc.definition: A. hard light

quizSrc.term: [img:Ti4mnGumEeeO-Q4_wz24ug_cafa22fe5186eeab1cf0ea3b8dfe146b_pexels-photo-239919.jpeg] A. rule of thirds B. soft light C. hard light D. cold light
quizSrc.definition: D. cold light

quizSrc.term: Every interaction is an opportunity to build your brand. This opportunities can change the image of your brand. Which of this two options is more important to keep a good reputation? A. The actions your brand makes. B. The promises your brand makes.
quizSrc.definition: A. The actions your brand makes.

quizSrc.term: The first step to build a brand consists in four key pillars: diferentiation, relevance, esteem and knoledge. Which is the appropriate meaning of ESTEEM? A. The reason for beeing a brand. The unique selling proposition. B. The extent of consumer awareness and understanding of its identity. C. Degree of reputation, respect and consideration. It includes quality. D. The appropiateness of a brand. The perceived importance of the brand.
quizSrc.definition: C. Degree of reputation, respect and consideration. It includes quality.

quizSrc.term: Behavioural segmentation is divided in occasions, beneficts, user status and usage rate. Choose the opction that corresponds with this definition: Dividing the market into groups according to how often consumers purchase. A. Occasions B. User status C. Beneficts D. Usage rate
quizSrc.definition: D. Usage rate

quizSrc.term: Which of the following descriptions correspond to A MOTIVATION? A. A state of deprivation B. They reduce needs C. They push people to resolve a problem D. Internal drivers E. Can be both physical and psycological
quizSrc.definition: C. They push people to resolve a problem E. Can be both physical and psycological D. Internal drivers

quizSrc.term: If we are talking about the iceberg model we have to think in four aspects: Behaviours, Needs, Tensions and Motivations. Which is the correct definition for tensions. A. What people satisfy by doing what they do. B. What people fear. C. The ultimate reason why people do what they do. D. What people do.
quizSrc.definition: B. What people fear.

quizSrc.term: Which are the four pillars to build a brand? A. a) Product, price, promotion, place. B. b) Differentiation, relevance, steem, and knowledge. C. c) Identity, differentiation, strategy, value.
quizSrc.definition: B. b) Differentiation, relevance, steem, and knowledge.

quizSrc.term: What is a positioning statement? A. a) A statement that summarizes the brand positioning decided by the organization. B. b) The claim used for advertising. C. c) The basic strategic goal of the organization.
quizSrc.definition: A. a) A statement that summarizes the brand positioning decided by the organization.

quizSrc.term: What is a brand house? A. a) A compilation of the different elements of the brands architecture. B. b) A blueprint for everything will be communicated about a brand. C. c) The backbone of the storytelling of a brand.
quizSrc.definition: A. a) A compilation of the different elements of the brands architecture.

quizSrc.term: How to build a brand personality? A. a) Defining its buyer personas. B. b) Defining the emotional triggers that people can connect with. C. c) Defining its visual identity.
quizSrc.definition: B. b) Defining the emotional triggers that people can connect with.

quizSrc.term: What are the main characteristics of a logo? A. a) Visible, different, reproducible. B. b) Different, clear, strong. C. c) Simple, memorable, timeless.
quizSrc.definition: C. c) Simple, memorable, timeless.

quizSrc.term: What does the term Cacophony mean ? A. a) An unpleasant mixture of loud sounds B. b) The simplification of headlines in search of traffic C. c) The proliferation of fake news
quizSrc.definition: A. a) An unpleasant mixture of loud sounds

quizSrc.term: What approximate percentage of online users use ad blockers to avoid more interruptions ? A. a) 10% B. b) 30% C. c) 60%

What is meant by niche audience? A. a) A huge group of people who have low interest in a particular subject B. b) A big group of people who have moderate interest in a particular subject C. c) A small group of people who have great interest in a particular subject
quizSrc.definition: C. c) A small group of people who have great interest in a particular subject

quizSrc.term: What action does the word ‘narrowcasting’ describe. A. a) The tailoring of content for individual people B. b) The dissemination of information to a smaller audience (instead of the broader public C. c) The concentration of media outlets
quizSrc.definition: B. b) The dissemination of information to a smaller audience (instead of the broader public

quizSrc.term: The main trend in branded content during the last decade is placing your audience’s emotions at the forefront, instead of focusing the message mainly on your product. True or false? A. True B. False
quizSrc.definition: B. False

quizSrc.term: Which is the main reason behind audience fragmentation, from a media business perspective? A. a) Political polarization B. b) The digital divide C. c) The exponential growth in content
quizSrc.definition: C. c) The exponential growth in content

quizSrc.term: What term did the Polish thinker Zygmunt Bauman coin to describe these times where’s “the growing conviction that change is the only permanence, and uncertainty the only certainty”? A. a) “Fake era” B. b) “Liquid modernity” C. c) “Virtual society”
quizSrc.definition: B. b) “Liquid modernity”

quizSrc.term: What is the most effective way of creating a valuable connection with a future customer? A. a) Producing expensive and high budget content marketing which focuses on the product B. b) Focusing on describing the brand values C. c) Focusing on the customers’ needs and interests
quizSrc.definition: C. c) Focusing on the customers’ needs and interests

quizSrc.term: What should be the primary definition of your online brand content narrative? A. a) Quick B. b) Relevant C. c) Visual
quizSrc.definition: B. b) Relevant.

quizSrc.term: Companies have a different content team or agency for each content channel. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: A. a) True

quizSrc.term: Free media coverage for relevant brand content is called... A. a) Earned media B. b) Owned media C. c) Liquid space
quizSrc.definition: A. a) Earned media

quizSrc.term: Which of the following traits are essential when producing brand journalism content? A. a) Several channels B. b) Creativity C. c) Verification
quizSrc.definition: C. c) Verification

quizSrc.term: Content syndication is an agreement by which an independent agency produces content for a big company inside their offices. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: B. b) False

quizSrc.term: Who bought (and saved) The Washington Post in 2013? A. a) Rupert Murdoch B. b) Elon Musk C. c) Jeff Bezos
quizSrc.definition: C. c) Jeff Bezos

quizSrc.term: The New York Times is a subscription-first newspaper. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: A. a) True

quizSrc.term: What is inverted pyramid style in journalism? A. a) Top heavy news story (the beginning contains the important information) B. b) Bottom heavy news story (the conclusion contains the most important truth) C. c) A style of writing based on community and human interest
quizSrc.definition: A. a) Top heavy news story (the beginning contains the important information)

quizSrc.term: In journalism, ‘convergence’ means: A. a) Sharing facilities, resources, staff or content B. b) Media concentration C. c) That the content of one newspaper is similar to many others because of financial constraints
quizSrc.definition: A. a) Sharing facilities, resources, staff or content

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is NOT true about journalism today? A. a) Editors in chief have less power than 15 years ago. B. b) Stories published by one site are likely to be found in other media. C. c) There are more fact-checkers than 25 years ago in newsrooms
quizSrc.definition: C. c) There are more fact-checkers than 25 years ago in newsrooms

quizSrc.term: There is only one age demographic group where roughly half of its population reads a daily newspaper: A. a) 20-25 years old B. b) 65 years or older C. c) Between 36 and 49
quizSrc.definition: B. b) 65 years or older

quizSrc.term: Despite the wave of criticism suffered by Facebook in recent years, it continues to be the highest source of news for the population of the United States. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: B. b) False

quizSrc.term: What does Inbound Marketing mean? A. a) A marketing technique which consists of ‘bouncing’ from one channel to the other in order to address different audiences B. b) A branding strategy which exclusively uses content produced internally by the company C. c) A strategy that utilizes several forms of pull marketing, blogs, events, SEO, social media… in order to create brand awareness
quizSrc.definition: C. c) A strategy that utilizes several forms of pull marketing, blogs, events, SEO, social media… in order to create brand awareness

quizSrc.term: Transmedia storytelling implies: A. a) No interaction from the user B. b) Some interaction from the user C. c) A high level of interaction from the user
quizSrc.definition: C. c) A high level of interaction from the user

quizSrc.term: Content curation is: A. a) ) Adding your own voice and value to a handpicked collection of content, gathered from a variety of sources that you publish and share online B. b) Filtering quality content out of a broader amount of content C. c) Distribution of quality content through any digital channel
quizSrc.definition: A. a) ) Adding your own voice and value to a handpicked collection of content, gathered from a variety of sources that you publish and share online

quizSrc.term: Which app is generally considered as a great example of how to use badges in order to engage users? A. a) Twitter B. b) Foursquare C. c) Facebook
quizSrc.definition: B. b) Foursquare

quizSrc.term: Gamification is more about keeping attention than attracting attention. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: A. a) True

quizSrc.term: Approximately how much branded content and games are viewed or enjoyed through smartphones? A. a) 80% B. b) 55% C. c) 30%

An effective tool for boosting engagement with very different types of content is adding a leaderboard. Leaderboards always track person-to-person or team-to-team competition. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: B. b) False

quizSrc.term: Which famous luxury brand has created a mobile game called to attract Chinese millennials called H-pitchhh? A. a) Guerlain B. b) Dior C. c) Hermes
quizSrc.definition: C. c) Hermes

quizSrc.term: LinkedIn’s progress bar when you complete your profile is a form of gamification. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: A. a) True

quizSrc.term: Augmented reality (AR) is considered the fastest-growing sector in gamification, offering an interactive experience of a real-world environment where real objects are ‘augmented’ by computer-generated multi-sensory perceptual information. What other names does it receive? A. a) Mixed reality B. b) Virtual reality C. c) Primary reality
quizSrc.definition: A. a) Mixed reality

quizSrc.term: What is meant by ¨Brand Personality¨? A. A famous person or celebrity who represents the Brand. B. The traits of the Brand if you imaged it as a person. C. The colors that the Brand uses. D. The logo of the Brand.
quizSrc.definition: B. The traits of the Brand if you imaged it as a person.

quizSrc.term: Why do we create Brand Personality statements and traits? A. To help us better understand what the essential elements of the Brand are. B. To convince the client that we know what we are talking about. C. To ensure that we don't copy any other Brand's ideas.
quizSrc.definition: A. To help us better understand what the essential elements of the Brand are.

quizSrc.term: What is the best way to phrase your Brand Personality Trait Description? A. In the third person, for example, ¨The Brand is very rebellious.¨ B. In the first person, for example, ¨I am very rebellious.¨ C. Or, for example, ¨You are very rebellious.¨
quizSrc.definition: A. In the third person, for example, ¨The Brand is very rebellious.¨

quizSrc.term: What can you use as inspiration for discovering Brand Personality Traits? A. Past campaigns by the competition. B. Characteristics of your favorite celebrity. C. Any information you can gather about the Brand. D. All of the above.
quizSrc.definition: D. All of the above.

quizSrc.term: What is a Brand Positioning Statement? A. A statement that the Brand makes to the press about its new activities. B. A Statement created by the Brand and its agency which defines what makes the Brand unique. C. A Statement which defines the countries in which the Brand is active.
quizSrc.definition: B. A Statement created by the Brand and its agency which defines what makes the Brand unique.

quizSrc.term: Why is it important to identify Brand Values? A. There's a lot of false advertising out there, so we need to know that your Brand doesn't engage in that. B. We need to know the Brand Value, or how much the company is worth, to decide if we want to work with them or not. C. Values define a Brand as much as its products, and Values connect to customers and clients at a deeper level.
quizSrc.definition: C. Values define a Brand as much as its products, and Values connect to customers and clients at a deeper level.

quizSrc.term: Mindmapping is a __________________________ technique? A. psychological analysis B. pointless C. medical D. brainstorming E. navigational
quizSrc.definition: D. brainstorming

quizSrc.term: Based upon the videos in this week, what is meant by the term ¨Creative Advertising Concepts¨? A. Ads which use clever but simple ideas to communicate what makes a Brand unique. B. Concepts which go beyond simply placing the product on the page. C. Campaigns which use tools like comparison, analogy, reference and emotional impact to stimulate the viewer. D. Concepts which make the viewer think a little, or a lot. E. All of the above.
quizSrc.definition: E. All of the above.

quizSrc.term: Mindmapping is a ___________________ technique. A. psychological analysis B. medical C. brainstorming D. navigational
quizSrc.definition: C. brainstorming

quizSrc.term: In the videos this week, we learned to use the technique of playing word associations for what purpose? A. When we want to come up with a new name for the company. B. When we are thinking of Brand Personality Traits or Creative Concepts. C. When we are trying to think of hashtags for a new viral campaign. D. When we want to convince our client to accept a challenging idea.
quizSrc.definition: B. When we are thinking of Brand Personality Traits or Creative Concepts.

quizSrc.term: True or False? Your campaign Concepts do not necessarily need to be linked to the Brand. A. True B. False
quizSrc.definition: B. False

quizSrc.term: In the ad that you see featured here, how would you best describe the main concept? [img:jlMYCGxgEee0ohLeDr_NVg_e3dd5d3c7e79fbd354a0cf0eb2f7cf1b_maxresdefault.jpg] A. Progress is the opposite of Madness. B. Progress divided by Madness equals a challenge. C. To make Progress, you have to take risks and be a little ¨mad.¨
quizSrc.definition: C. To make Progress, you have to take risks and be a little ¨mad.¨

quizSrc.term: The purpose of creating a Production Brief is: A. To show to the client yo can execute the project. B. To properly budget the project you are proposing. C. To communicate the execution of the project to your production team. D. All of the above.
quizSrc.definition: D. All of the above.

quizSrc.term: Of the following, which one is usually NOT included in a Production Brief? A. Mood Board B. Names of every member of the production crew. C. Makeup and hair options D. talent
quizSrc.definition: B. Names of every member of the production crew.

quizSrc.term: True or false? Branding is only communicated through the final retouching of an image, and not in things like the styling, hair and makeup or set design. A. True B. False
quizSrc.definition: B. False

quizSrc.term: What does ¨look and feel¨ refer to? A. The focus on the eyes of the model. B. The colors, tones, lighting, emotions and style of an image or campaign. C. A campaign which asks customers to see and then touch something. D. None of the above.
quizSrc.definition: B. The colors, tones, lighting, emotions and style of an image or campaign.

quizSrc.term: What is the ¨stylist¨responsible for? A. Selection of the clothing, accessories, and other physical elements of the shoot. B. Creating the hair and makeup for the models or actors. C. Retouching of the photos in post-production. D. None of the above.
quizSrc.definition: A. Selection of the clothing, accessories, and other physical elements of the shoot.

quizSrc.term: What’s the main reason for doing a creative pitch? A. a) Setting the basic creative ideas for the brand. B. b) Building a deep relationship with the client. C. c) Brainstorm about the brand identity.
quizSrc.definition: A. a) Setting the basic creative ideas for the brand.

quizSrc.term: What is a brand positioning statement? A. a) The claim used in the advertisements of a brand. B. b) A message that the brand transmits to the media in order to define its positioning. C. c) A statement created by the brand that defines what makes the brand unique.
quizSrc.definition: C. c) A statement created by the brand that defines what makes the brand unique.

quizSrc.term: What are the main elements to consider for developing a creative concept for a client? A. a) Who are the main competitors and their positioning. B. b) What is its problem and the possible solution. C. c) What are the needs and expectations of its audience.
quizSrc.definition: B. b) What is its problem and the possible solution.

quizSrc.term: Which are the elements included on a mood board? A. a) The emotional reactions you want to produce on your audience. B. b) The images used as a reference for the creative process of developing a campaign. C. c) The functions of the different members who will work on the production of the campaign.
quizSrc.definition: B. b) The images used as a reference for the creative process of developing a campaign.

quizSrc.term: What does “look and feel” refer to? A. a) The focus on the eyes of the model. B. b) The colors, tones, lighting, emotions, and style of an image or campaign. C. c) A campaign which asks customers to see and then touch something.
quizSrc.definition: B. b) The colors, tones, lighting, emotions, and style of an image or campaign.

quizSrc.term: Branded content creation can be effective at reinforcing the brand messages, but which of the following is also true? A. Branded content doesn't work well as communication for new customers B. Branded content typically reinforces the brand messages at the Awareness or Consideration phase, but less at later stages of the Customer journey C. Branded content does not really make an impact as customers make their purchase decisions based on features D. Branded content tends to dominate the customers' perceptions of the company at all stages and touchpoints throughout the journey
quizSrc.definition: B. Branded content typically reinforces the brand messages at the Awareness or Consideration phase, but less at later stages of the Customer journey

quizSrc.term: True or False: Most companies' CX investments emphasize the following activities: Cross-Departmental reach Qualitative and Quantitative Research Rigorous Analysis Well-established approaches to Design A. True B. False
quizSrc.definition: B. False

quizSrc.term: Which of the following explains why those responsible for different touchpoints along the customer journey do NOT apply brand guidelines? A. Some colleagues, such as those involved in the billing process, have a hard time connecting and applying brand guidelines to their activities. B. Brand guidelines are irrelevant for certain departments involved with customer touchpoints, who must apply their own contexts to be successful. C. The employee might be a new hire or has arrived via an acquisition, and is unaware of the existence of the brand guidelines. D. Both A and C.
quizSrc.definition: D. Both A and C.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is NOT an approach to helping to ensure the awareness and application of brand guidelines across departments? A. The application of brand guidelines should be linked to each team's quarterly objectives B. Involve other teams in the definition of the Brand Guidelines C. Use creative ways to spread the awareness of the guidelines such as quizzes, awards for brand champions, etc. D. Use brand guidelines as a way to start an ongoing conversation across different teams about how to best apply the guidelines in their contexts.
quizSrc.definition: A. The application of brand guidelines should be linked to each team's quarterly objectives

quizSrc.term: Why is it important to identify "Branding Strategy Orphans"? A. It's important to call out teams ignoring the brand to management. B. Brand Strategy orphans enables you to identify new opportunities for differentiation across the customer journey. C. Brand Strategy orphans enables you to identify and prioritize those touchpoints that are most disconnected from your Brand messages D. The Brand Strategy Orphans are an effective way to highlight missing brand messages throughout digital touchpoints, but less so for physical touchpoints.
quizSrc.definition: C. Brand Strategy orphans enables you to identify and prioritize those touchpoints that are most disconnected from your Brand messages

quizSrc.term: Which of the following reasons explain why CX metrics may introduce problems in executives' perceptions CX activities? A. Because executives tend to rely on data that might contradict your CX metrics. B. Because quantitative measures tend to describe where a customer problem might be, but not why the problem exists C. Because CX metrics have little relation to the financial success of a company. D. Because executives typically don't understand CX, they should not be monitoring CX activities.
quizSrc.definition: B. Because quantitative measures tend to describe where a customer problem might be, but not why the problem exists

quizSrc.term: Despite their limitations, CX teams should still pursue CX metrics projects. Which is NOT a reason we discussed in class? A. CX metrics are the most important metrics a company should track. B. CX metrics that are part of executive dashboards improve the visibility for the importance of CX activities C. CX metrics projects enable CX teams to demonstrate the impact of CX activities over time D. CX metrics projects enable CX team to connect their activities to business success metrics such as Churn, New Customer Acquisition, Cost of Customer Acquisition, and ultimately Revenue.
quizSrc.definition: A. CX metrics are the most important metrics a company should track.

quizSrc.term: Why is it important to deliver CX metrics projects in stages? A. It is the best way to secure funding. B. CX metrics need to evolve as dashboard users' questions evolve C. Because your colleagues never know what they want from their CX metrics and dashboards. D. It tends to be cheaper to deliver the CX metrics project in stages.
quizSrc.definition: B. CX metrics need to evolve as dashboard users' questions evolve

quizSrc.term: True or False? It makes sense to present qualitative research as part of CX dashboards? A. True B. False
quizSrc.definition: A. True

quizSrc.term: What is one of the risks introduced by CX metrics projects discussed in class? A. Over time, teams may start to focus more on affecting the numbers in the dashboard rather than the underlying Customer Experience. B. Customers data will be hacked and this will erode trust with the company and brand. C. The high visibility of CX metrics displayed in Executive Dashboards will lead them to put too much pressure on the CX organization to fix problems. D. If CX metrics improve over time, executives will feel less need to fund CX activities.
quizSrc.definition: A. Over time, teams may start to focus more on affecting the numbers in the dashboard rather than the underlying Customer Experience.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is an example of a CX metrics related to Customer Perceptions? A. Functional Completeness B. Net Promoter Score C. Customer Churn D. Number of support calls around a given issue .
quizSrc.definition: A. Functional Completeness

quizSrc.term: In the article, "Four ways to shape customer-experience measurement for impact" which reflects McKinsey's view on changing the mindset of CX measurement A. Define "owners" for specific customer journeys that bring together cross-functional teams. B. Implement journey dashboards to enable tracking journey events C. Enable "owners" to be able to drill down on dashboard data to see the details. D. All of the above
quizSrc.definition: D. All of the above

quizSrc.term: In the article, "How to Measure Customer Experience" from Gartner, which of the following metric categories is almost always ignored by Companies wishing to implement CX metrics programs? A. Employee engagement with the brand B. Customer Satisfaction C. Customer Churn D. Advocacy
quizSrc.definition: A. Employee engagement with the brand

quizSrc.term: Despite the evidence that Chatbots will, in general, improve Customer Experience, why should CX teams be cautious about their implementation? A. A) Customers may resent losing human contact B. B) Certain chatbot scenarios, e.g. making large financial transactions, will make some customers uncomfortable C. C) In at least some scenarios, chatbots will not be able to provide a relevant response D. D) Some customer conversations involve emotions, which chatbots generally cannot achieve E. E) All of the above.
quizSrc.definition: E. E) All of the above.

quizSrc.term: Why might the Call Center metric "First Call Resolution Rate" sometimes harm Customer Experience? A. A) Because the metric doesn't measure how well the call center employees solved the problem, just that the issue was closed. B. B) Because Average Call Duration is a better metric. C. C) Because sometimes customers prefer multiple calls and conversations to solve their problems. D. D) Because the number of calls is so variable from day to day, the rate is not a significant measure
quizSrc.definition: A. A) Because the metric doesn't measure how well the call center employees solved the problem, just that the issue was closed.

quizSrc.term: What could be the effect on CX if the Call Center Manager declares that her team will do "whatever it takes" to solve customer issues? A. A) It may be too expensive for the business to do so. B. B) Resolving issues may take so long that other callers are forced to stay on hold for long periods. But consider other responses. C. C) The Call Center team is not responsible for solving all customer problems, sometimes the solutions are owned by other teams. D. D) A&B only E. E) A, B and C
quizSrc.definition: E. E) A, B and C

quizSrc.term: Which below best explains the reasons why teams responsible for different customer touch points fail to reflect the official brand messages in their communications? A. A) Many colleagues have personalities that are self-absorbed and don't care about the rest of the company. B. B) Most customer-facing colleagues work in departments that don't require knowledge about the brand messages. C. C) Many colleagues simply don't know about the brand messages or their application to the customer-facing activities. D. D) Customer-facing teams often have very strong short term incentives that may conflict with the brand messages E. E) Both c and d
quizSrc.definition: E. E) Both c and d

quizSrc.term: Which best explains why Brand messages are often not effectively communicated in various customer touchpoints? A. The Brand Message is typically relevant only for the Awareness phase. B. Employees in charge of touchpoints don't need to know about high level brand strategy and the brand messages. C. Employees' incentives are misaligned with what is necessary to communicate the brand message. D. Training employees involved with touchpoints on brand messaging is not worth the Return on Investment
quizSrc.definition: C. Employees' incentives are misaligned with what is necessary to communicate the brand message.

quizSrc.term: From the reading "A FINE IS A PRICE", which below explains why should Branding and CX teams pay attention to the fee structure for services? A. A) Price increases enable the company to make more money which, in turn, enables more Branding and CX budget. B. B) Variable fees, such as charging parents for arriving late, enable the brand to signal good and bad behavior to customers C. C) Changes to prices and fees can negatively impact the customers' perceptions of and relationships with the brand D. E) Prices and fees should be detailed on the invoices, and not hidden
quizSrc.definition: C. C) Changes to prices and fees can negatively impact the customers' perceptions of and relationships with the brand

quizSrc.term: Regarding research, which below would be a good reason for CX teams to build alliances with UX teams? A. UX teams typically specialize in eye-tracking research that can benefit the Web design as a primary touch point B. UX teams, because they are in the product group, tend to have a larger budget than CX teams. This larger budget can be used to do more CX research. C. CX teams are often trained in ethnographic and other qualitative research methods, which enables their research to complement the CX team's more quantitative-based research inputs such as surveys. D. Since UX teams are typically involved with designing digital products - from website to software - the CX team can tell them what customers really want.
quizSrc.definition: C. CX teams are often trained in ethnographic and other qualitative research methods, which enables their research to complement the CX team's more quantitative-based research inputs such as surveys.

quizSrc.term: Why is it important to distinguish between "buyers" and "users" regarding research? A. Buyers' decisions to purchase are often based on different criteria than the product's Users, which can create misalignment with Brand Messages B. Buyers are better at understanding the "true" needs of the users than the users themselves are able to understand C. Because the "buying" decision comes prior to the USE Stage, it's more important to understand the "Buyers" D. Since the "Buyers" are the ones with the money and the authority to make the purchase, research on their preferences and expectations is the most important
quizSrc.definition: A. Buyers' decisions to purchase are often based on different criteria than the product's Users, which can create misalignment with Brand Messages

quizSrc.term: For digital products (mobile applications, software, etc), why do Product Teams resist implementing new branding guidelines? A. The USE Stage is not as important for the guidelines as the Awareness and Consideration stages B. Brand Guidelines are more appropriate for traditional marketing channels like brochures and the website than they are for digital products C. Because customers don't make USE decisions based on the brand, there is little incentive for Product Teams to spend time implementing brand guidelines in their digital products D. Brand guidelines applied to existing products can imply dramatic development costs that prevent the delivery of new features
quizSrc.definition: D. Brand guidelines applied to existing products can imply dramatic development costs that prevent the delivery of new features

quizSrc.term: Some CX roles have, as a primary focus, responsibility for Customer Support/Service. Which of the following reasons explain why this may lead to a worse CX? A. The Customer Support/Service function is often prioritized to reduce costs and improve operational efficiency, rather than improve CX B. Rather than solve customer issues, some Customer Support/Service teams focus on selling new products and services to Customers C. Customer Service/Support is driven to improve efficiency metrics that may not have a positive impact on Customer Experience D. All of the above
quizSrc.definition: D. All of the above

quizSrc.term: Why is "Peak End Rule" relevant for CX teams? A. A) The related experiments demonstrate the customers do their best to avoid painful or uncomfortable situations. B. B) It provides evidence that the CX touchpoints that cause the most customer pain should be the ones that CX teams address first. C. C) It shows how CX activities and projects should be linked to well-designed and rigorous experiments D. D) It shows that emphasizing a great experience for certain touch points may override a poor or neutral experience in other touchpoints. E. E) All of the above
quizSrc.definition: D. D) It shows that emphasizing a great experience for certain touch points may override a poor or neutral experience in other touchpoints.

quizSrc.term: According to the reading "Building a Brand on the Touchpoints That Count", like many companies banks have a wide range of options for prioritizing their CX efforts. Why might banks chose to improve the resolution of customer problems (e.g. better call center) rather than improving the workflows for placing an automatic transaction? A. Because resolving customer problems has a higher upside concerning the bank's reputation B. Because resolving customer problems are easier to measure and monitor. C. Because improving the ability to resolve customer problems is less expensive. D. Because fewer and fewer customers are using automatic transfers these days.
quizSrc.definition: A. Because resolving customer problems has a higher upside concerning the bank's reputation

quizSrc.term: Customer Experience and User Experience both care about the design of the experiences for their customers. According to the reading "customer-experience-vs-user-experience-why-the-difference-matters", in what way is Customer Experience different than User Experience? A. User Experience can be thought of a component of Customer Experience. B. A poor Customer Experience is devastating to a business while a bad User Experience is easily overcome. C. Branding Messaging is relevant for Customer Experience personnel, but User Experience focuses on digital products unrelated to the brand. D. Customer Experience professionals are generally better trained at design than User Experience professionals
quizSrc.definition: A. User Experience can be thought of a component of Customer Experience.

quizSrc.term: oPower has a unique approach to helping to shape the behavior of utility (gas/electric) customers. If a Hotel wanted guests to reuse towels rather than have cleaning staff automatically replace them with new ones, which message is most similar to oPower's approach? A. Help save the environment! Please hang your towels to reuse them. B. If our guests would reuse their towels across 2 days, it would save 10,000 gallons of detergent. Please hang your towels to reuse them. C. 87% of our guests respect the environment by reusing towels. Will you join them? Please hang your towels to reuse them. D. Why wash towels that are not dirty? Please hang your towels to reuse them.
quizSrc.definition: C. 87% of our guests respect the environment by reusing towels. Will you join them? Please hang your towels to reuse them.

quizSrc.term: What can we learn about Customer Experience from the diagrams describing the purchase workflow? A. We can learn that good CX practice can define how many times a customer is reminded to pay its bill. B. reflecting the experience from the customer's perspective. This gap can help identify areas for improvement. C. We can evaluate the effectiveness of the different touchpoints involved with the customer's Purchase stage and thereby assess the Customer Experience. D. We can see if there is a step missing that ought to be included but isn't (e.g. if another touchpoint would help increase customer satisfaction).
quizSrc.definition: B. reflecting the experience from the customer's perspective. This gap can help identify areas for improvement.

quizSrc.term: Regarding chatbot projects and their impact on customer experience, is it problematic to view "cost savings" as the primary goal? A. Yes, because chatbot technology can be very expensive, chatbots often increase costs. B. Yes. Because Chatbots can, if not carefullly designed, introduce significant CX issues. C. No, using Chatbots to save costs in one area, like Customer Service, enables the company to invest in more profitable areas. D. No. The "self-help" that Chatbots enable always improves CX over interventions by humans
quizSrc.definition: B. Yes. Because Chatbots can, if not carefullly designed, introduce significant CX issues.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following explains how design teams might use the Pareto Principle to help them in their job? A. By prioritizing the 20% of the features used by 80% of the users, the Pareto Principle helps Design teams focus and prioritize their efforts. B. The Pareto Principle allows Design teams to focus a little bit of effort on many small product capabilities. C. The Pareto Principle can be used to predict which product capabilities are most valuable to customers D. The Pareto Principle helps Design teams to identify the 20% of product capabilities to focus on and the 80% of those to drop
quizSrc.definition: A. By prioritizing the 20% of the features used by 80% of the users, the Pareto Principle helps Design teams focus and prioritize their efforts.

quizSrc.term: According to our discussions, which is a main cause of the friction between CX teams and Product Design/UX teams? A. UX teams don't care about the business impacts of their designs B. Each team brings separate research that provides different insights into understanding customers C. CX and UX teams tend to work on the same customer touchpoints in parallel but without coordination D. CX teams are more sensitive to the needs of customers while UX teams tend to focus on icons, colors and visuals
quizSrc.definition: B. Each team brings separate research that provides different insights into understanding customers

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is true about Customer Service (i.e. Call Centers) with regards to Customer Experience? A. Customer Service is the single most important determining factor for a great Customer Experience. B. Because delighting customers guarantees loyalty, the best Customer Service teams should spare no expense to solve all customer issues C. Customer Service should avoid metrics that create incentives to close out issues at the expense of customer satisfaction D. Regarding the budget for Customer Service personnel, Chatbots provide the best approach to reducing costs.
quizSrc.definition: C. Customer Service should avoid metrics that create incentives to close out issues at the expense of customer satisfaction

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is an important consideration when using Chatbots to deliver a great Customer Experience? A. CX teams should recognize that Chatbots always deliver superior CX than Live Chat, Email or Phone support B. CX teams should always consider how to identify and measure chatbot conversations that were successful versus those which were problematic. C. Chatbots enable CX teams to remove the messy and unpredictable emotional component from customer conversations D. By using historical data about customers and their interactions with the company, Artificial Intelligence enables chatbots to deliver the exact customer needs during each chatbot conversation
quizSrc.definition: B. CX teams should always consider how to identify and measure chatbot conversations that were successful versus those which were problematic.

quizSrc.term: The Value Proposition Canvas is a normally used to help understand how a company's products and services support its customers' explicit and implicit needs. In the context of our CX course, it can be used to A. A) Understand how your CX activities might bring value to colleagues and departments inside your company. B. B) Define which touchpoints have the biggest impact on customers C. C) Redefine the activities you and your CX team might do to better support your colleague's activities D. D) Detail your ideas about how your colleagues should contribute to a great Customer Experience
quizSrc.definition: A. A) Understand how your CX activities might bring value to colleagues and departments inside your company.

quizSrc.term: What role might Customer Experience professionals play in the design of Employee Experience? A. A) Because the Customer Experience team should be responsible for defining and putting in place a great Employee Experience B. B) The CX team management should simply focus on the employee experience of its team C. C) By using similar tools and methodologies as those used for CX design, Customer Experience designers are well-suited to help Human Resources define a great Employee Experience D. D) The Customer Experience team should help define the employee satisfaction surveys E. E) Both B and C
quizSrc.definition: E. E) Both B and C

quizSrc.term: According to the article "Employee experience: the new human resource management approach", which of the following are true. A. When designing an Exmployee Experience, it is important to Iterate and Experiment B. To deeply understand your Employees, surveys are not enough. Other methodologies should be used, such as deep interviews. C. It is important the recognize that the outcome of designing an Employee Experience is a new process for continuously investigating Employees and their needs D. All of the above
quizSrc.definition: D. All of the above

quizSrc.term: In the context of designing a great CX, why is it important to learn about your colleagues' roles, responsibilities and incentives? A. Before designing the Customer Experience, CX Teams need to work with other departments to understand the context of how they interact with customers B. They might have budget that can help your CX team do research C. Demonstrating empathy with your colleagues by asking about their role makes them more likely to do what you need them to do D. It provides you the opportunity to teach them how to use the Value Proposition Canvas
quizSrc.definition: A. Before designing the Customer Experience, CX Teams need to work with other departments to understand the context of how they interact with customers

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is one of the keys to designing a great Employee Experience A. Providing fun activities such as pingpong and Foosball tables B. Free taxi service for employees working late C. Recognizing that employees go through various stages during their career and that these stages require different touchpoints and outcomes D. Getting inspirations from successful technology companies which invest in Employee Experience in order to retain employees
quizSrc.definition: C. Recognizing that employees go through various stages during their career and that these stages require different touchpoints and outcomes

quizSrc.term: How can research help to understand Employee Experience be connected to Brand Strategy? A. Employee Satisfaction Surveys are a great way to reveal how effectively employees communicate brand messages B. Using an Employee Journey Map can be used to visualize how employees are engaged with the brand C. You can use the Value Proposition Canvas to learn which employees are not relaying the brand messages. D. The Brand team should participate in all of the interviews with Employees during research on Employee Experience
quizSrc.definition: B. Using an Employee Journey Map can be used to visualize how employees are engaged with the brand

quizSrc.term: Many companies outsource at least part of their customer experience to 3rd parties. Why might this lead to customer experience risks? A. Outsourcing the control of customer touchpoints along the journey can limit the ability to inject brand messages to those touchpoints. B. Companies who provide outsourcing services are not as good at delivering customer experience as your company C. Because customers want one single company to deliver all aspects of their experience with a product or service. D. When your company outsources to 3rd party companies, it signals to customers that the company has problems delivering parts of its operations, which can lower confidence in the company's ability to be successful.
quizSrc.definition: A. Outsourcing the control of customer touchpoints along the journey can limit the ability to inject brand messages to those touchpoints.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is NOT a factor used when considering whether to outsource the Customer Research activities? A. Working with a very well-known Customer Research and Design firm can help get credibility with management B. Outsourcing can help reduce the risk that your research teams might be biased or predisposed to certain research outcomes C. The research might demand methodologies or other expertise that your internal research team does not have D. Outsourced researchers have more flexibility to go on time-consuming customer visits
quizSrc.definition: D. Outsourced researchers have more flexibility to go on time-consuming customer visits

quizSrc.term: When outsourcing customer research to 3rd parties, which of the following might get lost? A. The ability for your team and other employees to build customer empathy by participating in the research B. Some of the key findings might be considered irrelevant, and thus discarded or ignored by the 3rd party research team. They can't possibly know about other contexts in your company where the research might be useful C. When connecting findings about Customer issues to solutions, the 3rd party researchers might not know about solutions your company may already have to address those findings. D. All of the above
quizSrc.definition: D. All of the above

quizSrc.term: What is one thing that your CX team might do to mitigate the possible losses from outsourcing customer research to 3rd parties? A. A) Hire a 3rd party researcher who also provides consulting to implement possible solutions to strategy, products, activities, etc. B. B) Ensure all stakeholders read the Consultants' final report and attend their final presentation. C. C) Because the Research consultant has the expertise, make sure they define the key milestones and deliveries along the way. D. D) Define an "Interaction Model" to detail how the CX team and colleagues will regularly interact with the Research Consultants throughout the research process. E. E) All of the above
quizSrc.definition: D. D) Define an "Interaction Model" to detail how the CX team and colleagues will regularly interact with the Research Consultants throughout the research process.

quizSrc.term: When considering outsourcing activities such as billing or customer support, which of the following can help mitigate the possible negative impact on Customer Experience? A. A) Secure an exclusive contract so that the 3rd party service provider only works with your company B. B) Learn about how the 3rd party service measures and tracks its customer experience C. C) Require that the 3rd party service provider regularly collects and shares CX metrics and their own research inputs with your company. D. B and C
quizSrc.definition: D. B and C

quizSrc.term: As discussed in the class, "Ethics matters part 1: Where is your ethical line?" in what way does ethics become personal for those designing customer experiences? A. It is important for individuals to avoid the participation in bribery activities B. Business goals such as getting customers to buy or use more of our products and services may come in conflict with a positive customer experience C. Designing customer experiences must represent the official brand messages regardless of the ability for touchpoints to back up the brand promise D. The ethical design of the customer experience should reinforce the idea that the customer is always right
quizSrc.definition: B. Business goals such as getting customers to buy or use more of our products and services may come in conflict with a positive customer experience

quizSrc.term: Imagine Company A is acquiring Company B. Company A has a different set of brand values than Company B. Why might this situation put particular pressure on ethical decision-making regarding the customer experience? A. The Customer Experience team in Company A won't care about Company B's customers. B. Company A will likely fire employees who don't agree with their brand values. C. If Company B's customers begin leaving because they perceive a significantly different Customer Experience, this may cause Company A to make false promises. D. There won't be much of an impact - most customers won't pay attention to changes in customer experience.
quizSrc.definition: C. If Company B's customers begin leaving because they perceive a significantly different Customer Experience, this may cause Company A to make false promises.

quizSrc.term: From our class, "Ethics matters part 2", what are some examples of the unethical use of data related to the Customer Experience? A. Launching surveys on customer samples which you know will lead to biased or unrepresentative responses B. Using anecdotes as a way to purposely mislead or misrepresent other data C. Presenting data that emphasizes the messages you want while hiding uncomfortable truths D. All of the above
quizSrc.definition: D. All of the above

quizSrc.term: What is the best way to fight against the misuses of or misinterpretations of quantitative data? A. Always ask for the original data sources so that you can do your own analysis. B. Ask about the data analysis credentials of those profiles presenting the results of data analysis and presentations C. Learn the basics of descriptive statistics and be ready to ask questions D. Be prepared for each meeting to respond to any use of metrics with your own interpretations of the data.
quizSrc.definition: C. Learn the basics of descriptive statistics and be ready to ask questions

quizSrc.term: In class, we spoke about how Artificial Intelligence and Predictive technologies can be used, not only to identify new products and services that correspond to customer profiles, but also products and services they shouldn't buy or don't use. What does this imply for CX? A. That customers are ignorant as to their wants and desires, and companies should help them B. In some cases, companies might sacrifice short term money to instead build longer term trust. C. We need to explicitly ask customers what they want at each stage of the customer journey D. The CX team needs to avoid revealing to customers that they might be paying too much or buying things they don't need.
quizSrc.definition: B. In some cases, companies might sacrifice short term money to instead build longer term trust.

quizSrc.term: Which are the main stages of a customer journey? A. a) Advertising, price, purchase, earning loyalty. B. b) Awareness, consideration, purchase, use, renewal. C. c) Selling channels, call centers, client satisfaction surveys.
quizSrc.definition: B. b) Awareness, consideration, purchase, use, renewal.

quizSrc.term: The main reasons for not creating good customer journeys are: A. a) They are not based on sufficient data and/or there is a lack of rigor in the data collection or the analysis and synthesis of the collected data. B. b) They do not involve the stakeholders responsible for the different CJM touch points. C. c) The implications or actions are either unachievable or overly focused on the short term. D. d) All of them.
quizSrc.definition: D. d) All of them.

quizSrc.term: What is the benefit of using the Value Proposition Canvas (VPC) for CX activities? A. a) Because CX teams must work across many teams, VPCs enable them to identify ways to connect the value of their activities to the success of other teams. B. b) It helps you define new activities that you should do that enable the other teams to be successful. C. c) VPCs help you identify other departments pains so that you can develop strategies for securing more budget.
quizSrc.definition: A. a) Because CX teams must work across many teams, VPCs enable them to identify ways to connect the value of their activities to the success of other teams.

quizSrc.term: What part of the customer journey do most brand activities emphasize over others? A. a) Awareness and consideration. B. b) Purchase. C. c) Use.
quizSrc.definition: A. a) Awareness and consideration.

quizSrc.term: The main function of a User Experience (UX) team are: A. a) Manage the customer support center. B. b) Define the digital products’ workflow and conduct research on customers’ preferences and usage context. C. c) Align the brand values with the uses of its audience.
quizSrc.definition: B. b) Define the digital products’ workflow and conduct research on customers’ preferences and usage context.

quizSrc.term: When analyzing a brand's history and past campaigns, what is the primary goal of the creative team? A. To ensure the new campaign looks exactly like previous successful videos and graphics. B. To identify the brand's unique selling proposition and evaluate its consistency over time. C. To choose a completely new color palette that contradicts the brand's historical identity. D. To skip the research phase and move directly to the production brief.
quizSrc.definition: B. To identify the brand's unique selling proposition and evaluate its consistency over time.

quizSrc.term: According to the process described, what is the relationship between a brand's "Unique Selling Proposition" (USP) and its Positioning Statement? A. The USP is a technical list of features, while the Positioning Statement is only for social media. B. The Positioning Statement is created first to tell the brand what its USP should be. C. The uniqueness found in the USP is used as the foundation to develop the brand positioning statement. D. They are the same thing and can be used interchangeably in the production brief.
quizSrc.definition: C. The uniqueness found in the USP is used as the foundation to develop the brand positioning statement.

quizSrc.term: Why is it important to emphasize looking at a target audience's internal motivators and values rather than just their age or gender? A. To understand the emotional drivers and values that actually trigger a purchase decision. B. Because demographic data like age and gender are no longer used in modern advertising. C. To make the brand personality analysis more complicated for the client. D. To determine which social media platform has the cheapest advertising rates.
quizSrc.definition: A. To understand the emotional drivers and values that actually trigger a purchase decision.

quizSrc.term: In the campaign strategy phase, how is the Strategic Objective distinguished from the Tactic? A. The Objective is "how" you do it; the Tactic is "what" you want to achieve. B. The Objective is the final creative idea, while the Tactic is the brand's history. C. The Objective is for the creative director, and the Tactic is only for the client. D. The Objective is the goal (e.g., increasing traffic), while the Tactic is the specific steps taken to accomplish it.
quizSrc.definition: D. The Objective is the goal (e.g., increasing traffic), while the Tactic is the specific steps taken to accomplish it.

quizSrc.term: What is the specific purpose of the Production Brief and the Mood Board in this process? A. To define the "look and feel," possible locations, and visual atmosphere of the proposed ideas. B. To list the names of every person who worked on the brand personality analysis. C. To replace the creative pitch entirely so the team doesn't have to present. D. To provide a technical summary of the brand's past financial performance.
quizSrc.definition: A. To define the "look and feel," possible locations, and visual atmosphere of the proposed ideas.

quizSrc.term: Every interaction is an opportunity to build your brand. This opportunities can change the image of your brand. Which of this two options is more important to keep a good reputation? A. The actions your brand makes. B. The promises your brand makes.
quizSrc.definition: A. The actions your brand makes.

quizSrc.term: The first step to build a brand consists in four key pillars: diferentiation, relevance, esteem and knoledge. Which is the appropriate meaning of ESTEEM? A. The reason for beeing a brand. The unique selling proposition. B. The appropiateness of a brand. The perceived importance of the brand. C. The extent of consumer awareness and understanding of its identity. D. Degree of reputation, respect and consideration. It includes quality.
quizSrc.definition: D. Degree of reputation, respect and consideration. It includes quality.

quizSrc.term: Behavioural segmentation is divided in occasions, beneficts, user status and usage rate. Choose the opction that corresponds with this definition: Dividing the market into groups according to how often consumers purchase. A. Usage rate B. User status C. Beneficts D. Occasions
quizSrc.definition: A. Usage rate

quizSrc.term: Which of the following descriptions correspond to A MOTIVATION? A. Can be both physical and psycological B. They push people to resolve a problem C. Internal drivers D. A state of deprivation E. They reduce needs
quizSrc.definition: B. They push people to resolve a problem C. Internal drivers E. They reduce needs

quizSrc.term: If we are talking about the iceberg model we have to think in four aspects: Behaviours, Needs, Tensions and Motivations. Which is the correct definition for tensions. A. What people satisfy by doing what they do. B. What people do. C. What people fear. D. The ultimate reason why people do what they do.
quizSrc.definition: C. What people fear.

quizSrc.term: Which of the following changes reflect the shift in power from traditional media gatekeepers to consumers in the 21st century? Select all that apply. A. Increase in user-generated content B. Rise of social media platforms C. Emergence of influencer marketing D. Concentration of media ownership E. Reduction in digital advertising.
quizSrc.definition: A. Increase in user-generated content B. Rise of social media platforms C. Emergence of influencer marketing.

quizSrc.term: Free media coverage for relevant brand content is called... A. a) Earned media B. b) Owned media C. c) Liquid space
quizSrc.definition: A. a) Earned media

quizSrc.term: Which of the following traits are essential when producing brand journalism content? A. a) Several channels B. b) Creativity C. c) Verification
quizSrc.definition: C. c) Verification

quizSrc.term: Content syndication is an agreement by which an independent agency produces content for a big company inside their offices. True or false? A. a) True B. False
quizSrc.definition: B. False

quizSrc.term: Who bought (and saved) The Washington Post in 2013? A. a) Rupert Murdoch B. b) Elon Musk C. c) Jeff Bezos
quizSrc.definition: C. c) Jeff Bezos

quizSrc.term: The New York Times is a subscription-first newspaper. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: A. a) True

quizSrc.term: What is inverted pyramid style in journalism? A. a) Top heavy news story (the beginning contains the important information) B. b) Bottom heavy news story (the conclusion contains the most important truth) C. c) A style of writing based on community and human interest
quizSrc.definition: A. a) Top heavy news story (the beginning contains the important information)

quizSrc.term: In journalism, ‘convergence’ means: A. a) Sharing facilities, resources, staff or content B. b) Media concentration C. c) That the content of one newspaper is similar to many others because of financial constraints
quizSrc.definition: A. a) Sharing facilities, resources, staff or content

quizSrc.term: Which of the following is NOT true about journalism today? A. a) Editors in chief have less power than 15 years ago. B. b) Stories published by one site are likely to be found in other media. C. c) There are more fact-checkers than 25 years ago in newsrooms
quizSrc.definition: C. c) There are more fact-checkers than 25 years ago in newsrooms

quizSrc.term: There is only one age demographic group where roughly half of its population reads a daily newspaper: A. a) 20-25 years old B. b) 65 years or older C. c) Between 36 and 49
quizSrc.definition: B. b) 65 years or older

quizSrc.term: Despite the wave of criticism suffered by Facebook in recent years, it continues to be the highest source of news for the population of the United States. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: A. a) True

quizSrc.term: What does Inbound Marketing mean? A. a) A marketing technique which consists of ‘bouncing’ from one channel to the other in order to address different audiences B. b) A branding strategy which exclusively uses content produced internally by the company C. c) A strategy that utilizes several forms of pull marketing, blogs, events, SEO, social media… in order to create brand awareness
quizSrc.definition: C. c) A strategy that utilizes several forms of pull marketing, blogs, events, SEO, social media… in order to create brand awareness

quizSrc.term: Transmedia storytelling implies: A. a) No interaction from the user B. b) Some interaction from the user C. c) A high level of interaction from the user
quizSrc.definition: C. c) A high level of interaction from the user

quizSrc.term: Content curation is: A. a) ) Adding your own voice and value to a handpicked collection of content, gathered from a variety of sources that you publish and share online B. b) Filtering quality content out of a broader amount of content C. c) Distribution of quality content through any digital channel
quizSrc.definition: A. a) ) Adding your own voice and value to a handpicked collection of content, gathered from a variety of sources that you publish and share online

quizSrc.term: Which app is generally considered as a great example of how to use badges in order to engage users? A. a) Twitter B. b) Foursquare C. c) Facebook
quizSrc.definition: B. b) Foursquare

quizSrc.term: Gamification is more about keeping attention than attracting attention. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: A. a) True

quizSrc.term: Approximately how much branded content and games are viewed or enjoyed through smartphones? A. a) 80% B. b) 55% C. c) 30%

An effective tool for boosting engagement with very different types of content is adding a leaderboard. Leaderboards always track person-to-person or team-to-team competition. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: B. b) False

quizSrc.term: Which famous luxury brand has created a mobile game called to attract Chinese millennials called H-pitchhh? A. a) Guerlain B. b) Dior C. c) Hermes
quizSrc.definition: C. c) Hermes

quizSrc.term: LinkedIn’s progress bar when you complete your profile is a form of gamification. True or false? A. a) True B. b) False
quizSrc.definition: A. a) True

quizSrc.term: Augmented reality (AR) is considered the fastest-growing sector in gamification, offering an interactive experience of a real-world environment where real objects are ‘augmented’ by computer-generated multi-sensory perceptual information. What other names does it receive? A. a) Mixed reality B. b) Virtual reality C. c) Primary reality
quizSrc.definition: A. a) Mixed reality
